summary: Soundboard recording with decent sound captures a scorching RFK show where the band pushed through heat‑induced fatigue, delivering a smoking “Playing In The Band” and an uplifting “Box of Rain”.
review: The patched soundboard source provides a listenable mix that many fans describe as ‘nice’.  The performance was mixed – Jerry slipped up at times but delivered a stellar PITB solo, the band soldiered through extreme heat, and Garcia later called the set “horrible”, yet audience members still recall highlights like “Box of Rain”.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is uneven (strong PITB and Box of Rain vs. Garcia’s own criticism and heat‑related sluggishness) while the recording is a usable soundboard with no major flaws, yielding an overall rating in the good‑to‑average range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note the mix is decent/nice; no specific complaints about clarity or drop‑outs, but no detailed technical praise either.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The scorching DC heat made the first set feel sluggish at points, yet the band’s grit kept a full 14‑song first set and a high‑energy second set highlighted by PITB.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Has occasional screw‑ups but is 'smokin'' on Playing In The Band.
band_member_comments.Phil: Questioned why he always sang at RFK; no specific playing comment.
